methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Anthrogen is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Ankit Singhal that has raised $4.5M to date. The valuation universe comprises 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies in Biotech & Health, including Tempus AI, Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and Freenome.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. Stage-Progression, anchoring on the $4.5M raised as a proxy for current value, applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next financing stage, yielding $11.3M. The Peer-Median approach could not be executed due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample size thresholds; while 16 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters produced three or more datapoints. Public-Comp Anchoring was inapplicable because Anthrogen's revenue is not on file, and private companies at seed stage rarely disclose top-line figures needed to apply the sector P/S median of 6.6x observed across public peers.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $10M to $13M, centered at $11M. This reflects limited triangulation depth. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific company's risk profile and the absence of revenue-based or peer-relative anchors. A more definitive valuation would require either disclosed financials or identification of narrower-stage comparables with known funding rounds.
